steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: When you see your friend in the morning, what do you say?
questions.hint: It's what we say when the sun comes up!

questions.question_text: Your teacher smiles and waves at you. What can you say?
questions.hint: It's a friendly word when you meet someone.

questions.question_text: When it's time to go home from school, what do you say?
questions.hint: It's what we say when we leave and go away.

items.definition: A greeting word
A casual greeting
To reply to a question
To hear attentively
A friendly word to greet someone
A short way to say hello
What you say when someone asks a question
Use your ears to hear carefully

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👋

steps.title: First Day Greeting
steps.text: He says, 'Hello!' She says, 'Hi!' They listen and answer the teacher.

items.explanation: Use 'is' with he, she, or it
This is correct! We use 'is' with he.

items.question: Who ___ he?
items.explanation: Use 'is' with he, she, or it

items.question: ___ is my friend.
items.explanation: Use 'He' as the subject of the sentence

steps.audio_text: Good morning, class! When I say 'hello', you must listen carefully. Then you can answer and say 'hi' back to me. Let's try it together!

questions.question: What does the teacher say first?
What must you do when the teacher speaks?
What can you say back to the teacher?

steps.prompt: Listen and repeat these sentences. Speak clearly!

prompts.prompt: Say: I say hi and answer the teacher.
prompts.expected_text: I say hi and answer the teacher.

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet someone?
Who ___ she?
When your teacher talks, you must ___.
True or False: 'Hi' is another way to say 'hello'.
When someone asks you a question, you ___.

steps.instruction: Let's talk about friends! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet a new friend?
questions.hint: We wave our hand and say this word when we meet someone.

questions.question_text: Where can you meet new friends?
questions.hint: This is a place where children learn and play together.

questions.question_text: What can you do with your friends?
questions.hint: Friends like to have fun and do activities with each other.

items.definition: Used to refer to two or more people
What someone is called
Not old or not known before
Having existed for a long time

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👥

steps.title: Ben and Kim
steps.text: Today, Ben and Kim join our class. They are new. Ben is happy. Kim is shy.

steps.explanation: Use he or she for one person; they for two.

items.sentence: The boy is at the table.
items.explanation: Use 'by' or 'next to' for position near the table

items.question: ___ is my new friend.
items.explanation: Use 'He' as the subject of the sentence

items.question: She has ___ new book.
items.explanation: Use 'a' before a singular noun starting with a consonant

steps.audio_text: Hello everyone! Today we have two new friends in our class. They are called Ben and Kim. Ben is seven years old. Kim is six years old. Let's say hello to our new friends!

steps.prompt: Practice introducing your friends! Say these sentences out loud.

questions.question_text: What does 'new' mean?
He ___ called Ben.
They ___ my friends.
Read: 'Ben and Kim are new. They are friends.' Who are new?
True or False: 'They' means more than one person.

steps.instruction: Let's learn about the alphabet! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do we use letters for?
questions.hint: We use letters when we write and read!

questions.question_text: Which one shows the ABC song start?
questions.hint: Think about the alphabet song you sing!

questions.question_text: What is your name's first letter called?
questions.hint: Names always start with a BIG letter!

items.definition: A set of letters
Say letters in order
A symbol in words
A group of letters

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 🔤

steps.title: The Spelling Game
steps.text: In class, they play a spelling game. She spells 'dog'. He spells 'cat'. They clap for the winner!

steps.rule_pattern: ___ is playing the game. Who is ___?

items.explanation: After 'can' we use the base verb

items.sentence: The book is at the table.
items.explanation: Use 'on' for things on top of a table

items.question: ___ is playing the game?
items.explanation: Use 'Who' to ask about a person

items.question: He is playing ___ game.
items.explanation: Use 'the' when we know which game

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Today we're learning the alphabet. Emma can spell her name: E-M-M-A. Tom can spell three short words: cat, dog, and pen. Who is good at spelling? They both are!

questions.question: How many words can Tom spell?
What are the children learning today?

steps.prompt: Practice speaking! Listen and repeat these sentences.

prompts.prompt: Say: She is learning the alphabet.
Say: He is playing the game. Who is he?
prompts.expected_text: He is playing the game. Who is he?

prompts.prompt: Say: They are spelling words together.

questions.question_text: What do we call A, B, C, D, E, F...?
She ___ learning letters.
When you say the letters in 'cat' (c-a-t), you ___ the word.
___ is playing the game? (Asking about a person)
True or False: 'Book' is a word made of letters.

steps.instruction: Let's review! Answer these questions about saying hello!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet someone for the first time?
questions.hint: We say this word when we want to greet someone and be friendly!

questions.question_text: Which word means to respond when someone asks you something?
questions.hint: When your teacher asks 'What's your name?', you give an _____.

questions.question_text: What's the opposite of 'old'?
questions.hint: When you get a toy you've never had before, it's _____!

items.definition: A friendly word to greet someone
What you say when someone asks a question
Use your ears to hear carefully
What people call you
Something you just got, not old
Say the letters in a word one by one
A, B, C are examples of this
Letters put together to make meaning
A short way to say hello
A word for two or more people
What people call you
Not new, been around a long time
All the letters from A to Z
Say each letter in a word
Pay attention with your ears
What you give when asked a question

items.question: What do you say when you meet a friend?
What do you do when your teacher asks you a question?
What do you use to hear sounds?
What word means the opposite of 'old'?
How many letters are in the alphabet?
When you say C-A-T, what are you doing?

steps.title: New Friends
steps.text: They are new. Hello! My name is Tim. Who is she? She is Ana. Let's listen and answer.

items.explanation: Use 'I' as the subject of a sentence
Use 'books' (plural) when there are two or more
After 'can' we use the base form of the verb
We need 'a' before a singular noun
Use 'He' as subject, and 'answers' for he/she/it

items.question: ___ name is Sarah.
items.explanation: Use 'My' before a noun to show possession

items.question: I have ___ old book.
items.explanation: Use 'an' before words starting with vowel sounds like 'old'

steps.audio_text: Hello class! My name is Miss Emma. Today we have two new students. Their names are Sam and Lily. Let's all say hello to them! Can you spell your name, Sam? Yes, that's right: S-A-M. Good job!

questions.question: How many new students are there?

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking! Say these sentences about yourself.

prompts.prompt: Say hello and tell me your name.
Can you spell your name? Say the letters one by one.
prompts.expected_text: My name is spelled [letters in your name].

prompts.prompt: Now make a full sentence. Say hello, your name, and spell it!
prompts.expected_text: Hello, my name is [your name] and I can spell it: [letters].

questions.question_text: What word means 'the letters from A to Z'?
___ are new students in our class.
Read: 'Hello! My name is Tom. I am 6 years old.' Who is speaking?
True or False: When you say the letters in a word one by one, you spell it.
Who is new? Answer: She ___ new.
